message:
security: core protocol hardening — canonical CBOR, durable store, registry binaries, webhook, SDK

Bundled follow-ups to the Gemini cross-lane audit that land in core
protocol crates rather than any single lane:

- crates/fcp-crypto/src/canonicalize.rs
  - Adds MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH = 128 guard so a pathologically
    nested CBOR value cannot drive unbounded recursion (DoS on
    canonical serialization).
  - Rejects non-finite floats (NaN / ±Inf) — per RFC 8949 §3.4.1 they
    have no deterministic canonical representation, and leaving them
    in produced signatures that could diverge across platforms.
  - Normalizes negative zero to positive zero so -0.0 and 0.0 canonicalize
    to the same byte stream (RFC 8949 §4.2.2).
  - Threads depth counter through canonicalize_value_in_place recursion.

- crates/fcp-store/src/durable.rs: DurableSymbolStore previously ran
  scrub_corrupt_symbols_locked and the used_bytes quota repair across
  two independent lock acquisitions in the `symbol` read path, so a
  concurrent durable write could observe the stale used_bytes between
  them and validate against quota that no longer reflected reality.
  Extracts the scrub+repair pair into scrub_object_if_present which
  keeps both mutations inside a single state-lock scope, and rewires
  symbol() to use it. Includes the missing ErrorKind import used by
  the new code path.

- crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs: Binary-artifact signature
  verification now rejects multi-linked binaries as a fresh
  LinkedBinary error. Previously a hardlink-farmed binary could
  pass the signature check while a parallel process swapped the
  target out from under the verifier (signed-content TOCTOU).
  Adds file_has_multiple_links() with unix / windows / fallback
  implementations and wires it into the post-signature metadata
  check so a nlink > 1 artifact fails before it can be executed.

- crates/fcp-webhook/src/signature.rs: Tightens webhook signature
  verification path (see diff for constant-time comparison and
  header-canonicalization updates).

- crates/fcp-conformance/src/compliance.rs: Extends compliance
  test matrix with the new canonicalization depth / non-finite-float
  rejection cases so conformance suites fail closed if the guard
  regresses.

- crates/fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs, crates/fcp-sdk/src/ratelimit.rs:
  Minor SDK-side adjustments aligning migration hook and rate-limit
  client surface with the updated core types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
